examples: She literally ran a marathon yesterday.

context: So we had to literally scoop the bread into the peanut butter jar. We were not prepared.

examples: That alley looks a bit sketchy.
I wouldn't eat there; it looks sketchy.
He sent me a sketchy link.
context: One of our tail lights was burned out and we had no idea. We said we're coming back from a long road trip. We didn't know. And he could have ticketed us, but he ended up letting us off with a warning. Just go to a car shop and fix it. He was really nice as well. Were you scared when the police officer came up? I was scared when I saw a huge cop. Sometimes on the news, oh my god, let's not move. I should not do stupid stuff here. I don't want to get shot. Actually, that is a good point. When a police officer does come to your window, you really should not move. You need to keep your hands on the wheel. And then if they ask for something like your license or registration, you should warn them. Oh, it's in my glove box. Can I reach for it? Or it's in my back pocket. Can I grab that quick? Um, just because with the gun culture, if you don't say anything and you just reach, it could be a little bit sketchy. So always hands on the wheel and warn them before you grab for something. Yes, sir. Do you have a license? Yes, I have. I have in my pocket, in my wallet. Can I bring it out and you have any problem? We got let off with the warning.

examples: I was struggling to keep up with the pace.
Many students are struggling with math.
She's been struggling financially this year.
context: Walmart security is sitting there waiting for us to leave. And we're struggling to get in the car, fighting over who had the keys. We had no choice but to call roadside assistance like our insurance to get some help. But all of that information of course was in the glove box of the car. So we didn't even have access to it. So what do we have to do? Call dad. Call dad for the second time that day. And the worst part was there's a huge time difference. There's a three hours difference.
